description_long: This is a multi-story corner pub named 'The Black Friar', featuring ornate architectural details and a prominent statue at the corner entrance. The facade is constructed from brown brick with white stone trim, and the ground floor is adorned with large windows and decorative signage. The roof is slightly sloped and appears to be made of metal, with some weathering visible. The upper floors have regularly spaced sash windows, some with small balconies or decorative railings. The main entrance is grand, with an arched doorway and sculptural elements, while the side and rear walls are plainer and less detailed. The building sits directly on the street, with no visible outdoor seating or garden area.
description: A historic, multi-story corner pub called 'The Black Friar' with ornate stonework, large windows, and a prominent statue at the entrance.
description_view_0: Corner view showing the main entrance with a statue, ornate stonework, and windows on both visible facades.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the secondary entrance, large ground-floor windows, and less decorative brickwork.
description_view_2: Rear view with plain brick walls, minimal windows, and no decorative features.
description_view_3: Front view displaying the main signage 'The Black Friar', large arched windows, and the primary entrance.
attribute_signage: Wall-mounted signage above the main entrance on the front facade, rectangular in shape, with gold lettering reading 'SALOON BAR 174 THE BLACK FRIAR' on a black background. The signage is large, spanning the width of the ground floor windows.
attribute_entrance_design: One main entrance located at the corner of the building, featuring an arched doorway with ornate stonework and a prominent statue above. The door appears to be a single, heavy wooden door with glass panels, framed by decorative columns and stone trim.
attribute_window_arrangement: Multiple sash windows arranged symmetrically on the upper floors, with large arched windows on the ground floor. Some upper windows have small balconies or decorative railings. The rear and side walls have fewer and smaller windows.
attribute_architectural_style: Edwardian with Art Nouveau influences, characterized by ornate stonework, sculptural elements, arched windows, and decorative signage.
